title: Christmas Card Competition 2025 - Lilian Greenwood MP
text_excerpt: Christmas Card Competition 2025 - Lilian Greenwood MP Skip to main content Home About Lilian Contact Lilian Apply for PMQs tickets Information for schools Christmas Card Competition 2025 Office Standards Policy Help and support Privacy Notice More Menu Christmas Card Competition 2025 . . The annual competition to design my official Christmas card has now closed and the winners have been decided! The cards featuring the winning design will be professionally printed and sent out to hundreds of individuals, organisations, and people that I’ve worked with, both in Nottingham and across the country. Three runners up have also been chosen, and their designs will feature on the back of the finished card. There are also prizes for runners up, as well as Special Merit awards and a ‘Best in School’ prize for each school taking part. The theme this year was local transport: cycling, walking, taxis and road safety. If any children struggled with the theme they were able to do a brightly designed Christmas card. . . The winning design has been chosen by a panel of local judges including: Tasha Markham – Operations Manager, E.ON Next; Richard Daniels , Founder, Forever Stars; Adam Hussain , Senior Manager, Technical Training & Development Specialist, E.ON Net Zero Academy; Lisa Brackner , People & Development Director, Bridgeway Consultants Ltd .; and Louise Gilmore – Policy Advocacy Manager, E.ON. I’m pleased to say that our main sponsor this year is EON, along with support from Nottingham Bowl, Nottingham Panthers, Notts County Cricket Club, Nottingham Forest FC, Notts County FC, and Theatre Royal. Sponsors donate the printing and postage of the cards, as well as prizes for the winners. . This year’s 2025 overall winner is Charlie Gu, age 10, from Fernwood Primary School in Wollaton. Overall Winner: Charlie Gu, Age 10, Fernwood Primary School 2025 Runner Up: Beauty Ayotunde, Age 7, Welbeck Primary School 2025 Runner Up: Teddy Simeonov, Age 10, Mellers Primary School 2025 Runner Up: Kieran Shaw, Age 15, Nethergate Academy Department for Transport Ministerial Card Winner: Adam Zeb, Age 7, Fernwood Primary School E.ON Winner: Dara Adeagbo, Age 11, Radford Academy Forever Stars Winner: Evelyn Jackson, Age 10, Greenfields Community School Bridgeway Consulting Winner: Theo Jones, Age 8, South Wilford Primary School . Last year I also sent a Christmas card in my capacity as the then Minister for the Future of Roads. Ministerial Card Winner: Grace Plant, Age 9, Fernwood Primary School. . 2
